zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane de Houghton於1355年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾薩爾福德時，她的父親Adam de Hoghton46歲，母親Ellen Venables44歲。她於大約1375年與Richard Richmond結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1417年在英國英格蘭爾易亞斯坦享年62歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Houghton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of numerous places called Houghton or Hoghton. Most of the placenames derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some have different etymologies; for example, Little Houghton in Eccles (Lancashire), which derives from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn, and Houghton in Bigbury (Devon), which may derive from an Old English personal name Huhha + Old English tūn.

variants.name: Haughton
meanings: English: habitational name from Haughton (Cheshire, Lancashire, Northumberland, Shropshire, Staffordshire), or Haughton le Skerne (Durham). The placenames derive from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .
Irish: adopted for Ó hEacháin ‘descendant of Eachán’, a pet form of the personal name Eachaidh (see Hoey ).
Irish: adopted for Ó hEachtair ‘descendant of Eachtair’, a personal name of uncertain origin. Compare Hoctor .

variants.name: Hufton
meanings: English: habitational name; probably from Stony Houghton in Pleasley (Derbyshire), the name of which derives from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jane de Houghton was born in 1355, in Salford, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Adam de Hoghton, was 46 and her mother, Ellen Venables, was 44. She married Richard Richmond about 1375. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1417, in Eastham, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane de Houghton est née en 1355, à Salford, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Adam de Hoghton, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Ellen Venables, avait 44 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Richmond environ 1375. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1417, à Eastham, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane de Houghton 1355 in Salford, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Adam de Hoghton 46 und ihre Mutter Ellen Venables 44. Sie hat um 1375 Richard Richmond geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1417 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Eastham, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane de Houghton è nata il nel 1355 nel Salford, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Adam de Hoghton, aveva 46 anni e sua madre, Ellen Venables, aveva 44 anni. Lei sposato Richard Richmond circa 1375. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1417, nel Eastham, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 62 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Jane de Houghton(이)가 1355년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 살퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Adam de Hoghton(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ellen Venables(은)는 44세였다. 그녀는 대략 1375년 Richard Richmond(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1417년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 이스섬에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane de Houghton родилась в 1355 г. в местности салфорд, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Adam de Hoghton было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Ellen Venables, 44. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Richmond около 1375 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1417 г. в местности эастам, чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет.
